skill.name: add-dependency
skill.description: Guides approved additions of third-party Gradle dependencies through version catalog updates and build verification.
skill.summary: Add approved Gradle dependencies with version catalog entries and build checks.
skill.author: AnySoftKeyboard

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md. The backtick execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown examples and prose, while the Gradle build instruction is a legitimate external command risk that should remain disclosed.

low_findings.title: External Build Command Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run ./gradlew assembleDebug or a relevant build task after adding a dependency. This is expected for dependency validation, but it executes project build logic and should be run only after user approval in a trusted workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly present in the workflow and has a legitimate validation purpose. Risk is limited because the skill requires user approval before dependency changes and does not hide the command.

low_findings.title: False Positive Markdown Backtick Detections
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged several Markdown backtick and code fence locations as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed content is documentation for search, TOML formatting, Gradle catalog edits, and version catalog guidance, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visibly Markdown code spans or a TOML example. No Ruby code, command interpolation, or hidden execution path is present in the only scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Signals
low_findings.description: The description and dependency necessity guidance were flagged as weak cryptography and system reconnaissance. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, host inspection, credential collection, or environment probing in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain workflow text about dependencies. They do not contain cryptographic algorithms, system commands, or reconnaissance instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command
dangerous_patterns.description: A Gradle build command is documented as a verification step. It is not malicious, but marketplace users should understand that build scripts can execute local project logic.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact command is present and its intent is build verification. The risk depends on the trusted state of the target project rather than hidden behavior in this skill.

content.value_statement: Adding dependencies can create version drift and unreviewed supply chain risk. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through approval, version catalog updates, and build verification.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a new third-party dependency is necessary before changes begin.
Prompts for user approval before adding a new dependency.
Guides lookup of the latest stable library version.
Recommends a Gradle build or sync check after the catalog update.
limitations: Does not choose dependencies without user approval.
Does not perform license, vulnerability, or provenance checks by itself.
Requires a Gradle project that uses a version catalog.
Build verification depends on the local project and available Gradle tasks.

use_cases.title: Add an Android Library
use_cases.description: Add a required Android dependency while keeping versions centralized in libs.versions.toml.

use_cases.title: Review Dependency Need
use_cases.description: Compare alternatives before introducing a third-party library to a shared codebase.

use_cases.title: Standardize Dependency Updates
use_cases.description: Follow a repeatable workflow for catalog edits and build validation in pull requests.

prompt_templates.title: Check Need First
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the add-dependency skill to evaluate whether we need a library for [task]. Explain alternatives and ask before editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Add Approved Library
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the add-dependency skill to add [library] after approval. Update the version catalog and show the files changed.

prompt_templates.title: Use Latest Stable Version
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the add-dependency skill to find the latest stable version of [library], then prepare the version catalog entry.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Build Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the add-dependency skill to add [library], run the relevant Gradle verification task, and summarize any build issues.

output_examples.output: The assistant explains why Retrofit may be needed, compares simple alternatives, requests approval, then prepares version catalog changes.

output_examples.input: We approved a dependency for image loading.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the latest stable release, updates the Gradle version catalog, and recommends a relevant build check.

output_examples.input: Verify this dependency addition before a pull request.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms catalog usage, checks for hardcoded versions, runs the selected Gradle task, and reports the result.

best_practices: Get explicit user approval before adding any third-party dependency.
Keep dependency versions in gradle/libs.versions.toml instead of build files.
Run a focused Gradle build or sync task after changing dependencies.
anti_patterns: Adding a library when a small local implementation is sufficient.
Hardcoding dependency versions directly in build.gradle.kts files.
Skipping build verification after changing dependency coordinates.

faq.question: Does this skill add dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the assistant to request user approval before modifying project files.

faq.question: Which build systems does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Gradle projects that use gradle/libs.versions.toml for dependency management.

faq.question: Can it find the latest library version?
faq.answer: Yes. It tells the assistant to search for the latest stable version before editing the catalog.

faq.question: Does it check security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No. Vulnerability, license, and supply chain checks require separate tools or review steps.

faq.question: Why does it run a build command?
faq.answer: The build command verifies that Gradle resolves the new dependency and the project still compiles.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
